title: How to Launch a Website in GoHighLevel — Pro Templates
description: Learn how to launch a professional website in GoHighLevel using pro templates or from scratch. Step-by-step guide with domain setup and publishing tips.
html_content:
Building a professional website used to mean hiring a developer, waiting weeks, and spending thousands of dollars. Not anymore. GoHighLevel's website builder puts full design control in your hands—no coding required. Whether you're a solo entrepreneur, digital agency, or service business, you can launch a stunning, conversion-focused website in minutes using our pro templates or start from a blank canvas. This guide walks you through the entire process, from accessing the website builder to going live with a custom domain. Ready to skip the complexity? Start your free 30-day GoHighLevel trial today and build your first site by tomorrow.

📋 In This Guide

  • How to Navigate to the Websites Section in GoHighLevel
  • Template vs. Blank Website: Which Should You Choose?
  • How to Launch a Website From a Template in GoHighLevel
  • How to Launch a Website From Blank in GoHighLevel
  • Customizing Your Domain and Publishing Your Website
  • Essential Website Customization Tips for Maximum Conversions

How to Navigate to the Websites Section in GoHighLevel

Your journey to a live website starts with finding the right place in GoHighLevel. The platform is intuitive, but knowing exactly where to go saves you time.

Log into your GoHighLevel account and look for the main navigation menu on the left side of your dashboard. You'll see several core sections: Contacts, Funnels, Campaigns, and more. Scroll down until you find Websites. Click it, and you're instantly in the website builder hub.

Inside the Websites section, you'll see a prominent button that says "Create Website" or "New Website." This is your entry point. Click it, and GoHighLevel presents you with two clear options: start from a template or build from scratch. Both paths lead to a professional website—the choice depends on your timeline and design preferences.

💡 Pro Tip

Bookmark the Websites section for quick access. You'll return here to edit, publish, and manage all your sites from one central dashboard. This is especially valuable if you're building sites for multiple clients.

Template vs. Blank Website: Which Should You Choose?

This decision matters. It affects how fast you launch and how much customization you do upfront.

Choose a Template If: You want to launch in minutes, not hours. GoHighLevel's pro templates are industry-specific (service businesses, e-commerce, coaching, real estate, etc.) and pre-built with sections for hero images, testimonials, contact forms, and calls-to-action. They're mobile-responsive by default and follow conversion best practices. You still customize colors, text, images, and content—but the structure is done.

Choose a Blank Website If: You have a specific vision that doesn't fit existing templates, or you want complete creative freedom from the start. Building from blank takes longer (1–3 hours instead of 15–30 minutes), but you control every pixel. This approach works best if you already know your design direction or have a reference site in mind.

Most agencies and solo entrepreneurs choose templates first, then customize heavily. It's the fastest path from zero to live with professional polish.

How to Launch a Website From a Template in GoHighLevel

Templates are your shortcut to a polished, conversion-ready website. Here's the exact process:

Step 1: Select Your Industry Template

After clicking "Create Website," you'll see a gallery of templates organized by industry. Scroll through and find one that matches your business type. Templates include Service Provider, SaaS, Course Creator, Real Estate Agent, Fitness Coach, Digital Agency, and more. Click the template that resonates with your brand.

Step 2: Review the Template Preview

GoHighLevel shows you a full-page preview of the template. Scroll through and see all sections—header, hero, features, testimonials, FAQ, and footer. Check that the layout matches your vision. You can always modify sections later, so don't overthink this step.

Step 3: Create and Name Your Website

Click "Use This Template" or "Create With Template." You'll be prompted to name your website (e.g., "My Service Business" or "Client ABC Website"). This name appears in your GoHighLevel dashboard; it's not your public domain name. Choose something you'll recognize if managing multiple sites.

Step 4: Enter the Website Editor

The visual editor loads with your template fully functional. You're now in the drag-and-drop builder. Every section—headline, image, text block, form, button—is clickable and editable. Click any element to change text, swap images, adjust colors, or delete sections you don't need.

Step 5: Customize Core Sections

Start with the essentials: update your headline and hero image, replace placeholder testimonials with real ones, customize the contact form, and update your service descriptions. GoHighLevel's editor is WYSIWYG (what-you-see-is-what-you-get), so all changes appear live as you type.

Step 6: Mobile Preview and Optimization

Before publishing, click the mobile preview icon (usually a phone icon in the toolbar) to see how your site renders on smartphones and tablets. GoHighLevel templates are mobile-first, but verify your custom images and text display correctly on small screens. Make adjustments if needed.

Step 7: Save and Publish

Click the "Publish" or "Save" button in the top-right corner. GoHighLevel saves your changes to a staging version. You're now ready to connect your domain (covered in the next section).

How to Launch a Website From Blank in GoHighLevel

If templates don't fit your vision, a blank canvas gives you complete control. Here's how to build from scratch:

Step 1: Create a Blank Website

In the Websites section, click "Create Website" and select "Blank Website" (or "Start From Scratch"). Name your site and confirm. GoHighLevel opens a completely blank editor with a single empty page.

Step 2: Set Up Your Homepage Structure

Begin by adding sections. Click the "+" or "Add Section" button and choose from pre-built section templates (hero, two-column, testimonials, form, etc.). You're not starting from zero—GoHighLevel provides reusable section components. This hybrid approach saves time while maintaining creative freedom.

Step 3: Add and Edit Page Elements

Each section is populated with placeholder text, images, and buttons. Click any element to edit it. Change headline text, upload your logo and images, adjust colors, and customize button text and links. The drag-and-drop interface lets you reorder elements vertically or horizontally.

Step 4: Create Additional Pages

Websites need multiple pages (Home, About, Services, Contact, etc.). Click "Add Page" in the page menu. Create a new page for each major section. GoHighLevel automatically builds navigation menus—you just manage which pages appear.

Step 5: Set Up Navigation

Open your header/navigation settings. Add links to your new pages. You can customize menu layout, colors, and positioning. Most agencies keep navigation simple: Home, Services (or About), Contact, and one optional CTA page.

Step 6: Test and Refine

Use the preview mode to see your site as visitors will. Check that all links work, forms function, and pages load correctly. Click through every button and form. This step catches issues before publishing.

Step 7: Publish Your Blank-Built Website

Click "Publish" when satisfied. Your site is now saved and ready for a domain.

Customizing Your Domain and Publishing Your Website

A website without a custom domain isn't truly live. GoHighLevel makes domain connection seamless:

Option 1: Use a GoHighLevel Subdomain (Free)

By default, your site gets a GoHighLevel subdomain (e.g., yourname.gohighlevel.com). This is free and functional, but not branded. It's fine for testing but suboptimal for professional use.

Option 2: Connect Your Own Domain

If you own a domain from GoDaddy, Namecheap, or another registrar, connect it to GoHighLevel. Go to your website settings and select "Connect Custom Domain." GoHighLevel provides DNS records you'll add to your domain registrar. This takes 10 minutes and costs nothing (domains are usually $10–15/year elsewhere).

Option 3: Purchase a Domain via GoHighLevel

Alternatively, buy a domain directly through GoHighLevel during setup. This streamlines the process and auto-connects your site.

Once your domain is connected and verified, your website is live and publicly accessible. Anyone can visit your custom URL and see your site in full.

Essential Website Customization Tips for Maximum Conversions

1. Optimize Your Headline for Your Audience

Your hero headline is the first thing visitors read. Make it benefit-focused, not generic. "Grow Your Agency Fast" beats "Digital Agency Website." A/B test different headlines later using GoHighLevel's built-in analytics.

2. Use High-Quality Images and Video

Replace all placeholder images with professional photos of you, your team, or your clients' results. Video heroes (auto-playing background videos) increase time-on-page by 40%+. Keep file sizes under 1MB for fast load times.

3. Add Clear Calls-to-Action (CTAs)

Every page should guide visitors toward one primary action: Book a Call, Download a Guide, Join the Community, Schedule a Demo. Use contrasting button colors and place CTAs at the top, middle, and bottom of pages.

4. Integrate Your Forms and Funnels

GoHighLevel websites integrate directly with your CRM and funnels. When visitors submit a contact form, their data automatically flows into your Contacts list. You can trigger automations, send emails, and nurture leads—all from form submissions on your site.

5. Enable SSL and Security

GoHighLevel includes free SSL certificates on all websites. Ensure this is enabled in your site settings. Visitors see a green lock next to your URL, which builds trust.

6. Monitor Analytics

GoHighLevel provides built-in analytics showing page views, visitor sources, and conversion rates. Check these weekly. If a page isn't performing, update the headline, images, or CTA and test again.
